subject: Project Management
title: The Panama Canal
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Panama Canal usable.

hook.text: France tried to build the Panama Canal and failed — 20,000 workers died, mostly from disease.

buildup.text: De Lesseps used the same approach as Suez: a sea-level canal. But Panama wasn't flat.

discovery.text: The U.S. took over in 1904, switched to locks, and conquered malaria with mosquito control.

twist.text: Chief engineer Stevens quit mid-project. His replacement finished it as a military operation.

climax.text: The canal opened in 1914 — one of the greatest engineering feats in history.

punchline.text: The project that killed one nation's dream built another's.

quiz.question: Why did France's attempt to build the Panama Canal fail?
options: Disease killed workers and the sea-level design was wrong
They ran out of funding immediately
The U.S. invaded and stopped them
